name: Bertelsmann SE & Co. KGaA
summary: Bertelsmann SE & Co. KGaA operates as a media, services, and education company. The company's RTL Group division engages in the broadcasting, content, and digital activities. It holds interest in 67 television channels, 10 streaming platforms, and 38 radio stations, as well as content production and digital video networks; and creates, produces, and distributes television content. Its Penguin Random House division publishes trade books with approximately 300 imprints in 6 continents. This division publishes 15,000 new titles; and sells 600 million print books, audiobooks, and e-books. The company's Gruner + Jahr division publishes Stern, Brigitte, Geo, Barbara, Eltern, Essen & Trinken, Guido, Sch√É¬∂ner Wohnen, Chefkoch, Beef, 11 Freunde, Spiegel-Gruppe, and Wohllebens Welt magazines; Prisma Media, a French magazine; and S√É¬§chsische Zeitung, a newspaper. It holds interests in Outbrain, an online native advertising market; and urbia.de, Chefkoch.de, and AppLike digital offerings. Its BMG division operates as a music publisher. It holds approximately three million songs and recordings. The company's Arvato division provides supply chain, financial, customer relations, and IT services. Its Bertelsmann Printing Group division provides illustration and book printing services, as well as digital printing of customizable products. This division produces magazines, catalogs, brochures, books, and packaging products. The company's Bertelsmann Education Group division provides digital education and services in healthcare and technology sectors, as well as e-learning services and online education platform. Its Bertelsmann Investments division invests in start-up companies in Brazil, China, India, the United States, and Europe. It operates in approximately 50 countries worldwide. The company was formerly known as Bertelsmann AG and changed its name to Bertelsmann SE & Co. KGaA in August 2012. Bertelsmann SE & Co. KGaA was founded in 1835 and is headquartered in G√É¬ºtersloh, Germany.
